title: Boat Rental in Cancun: 2026 Costs, Boats & Best Departure Points
html:
Booking a boat in Cancun, Mexico, opens up the turquoise waters of the Caribbean Sea and the Nichupté Lagoon. You can rent a motor yacht from the Puerto Cancún Marina for around 400 USD for a half-day trip. BoatHire24 makes it easy to compare verified listings and secure your ideal vessel.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cancun?

A shared tour starts at around 80 USD and a private yacht can exceed 2,000 USD per day. The final price depends on the boat size, trip duration, and whether you want a skipper or crew. Expect to pay 150-300 USD per person for a full-day catamaran trip including lunch and open bar. For a private charter of a 12-metre motorboat, the half-day cost is roughly 600-800 USD. For a full-day crewed yacht with a capacity of 10-12 guests, prices typically range from 1,500 to 2,500 USD. All prices are in USD, the local currency for these rentals. For a wide selection of skippered charters in Cancun, browse skippered charters in Cancun on BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Cancun

The main departure points are the Puerto Cancún Marina, Marina Punta Estrella, and the public dock at Playa Tortugas. For trips to Isla Mujeres, the ferry pier in Puerto Juárez also offers private boat pick-ups. Many charters leave from the Club de Yates de Cancún, a private marina near the Hotel Zone. To explore the coastline south, consider departing from the Marina at Puerto Morelos, a charming fishing village about 20 nautical miles from Cancun centre. The catamaran dock at Embarcadero Isla Mujeres is another busy hub for shared excursions.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor yacht (30-60 ft) ideal for luxury groups of 8-12; from 700 USD half-day.
  2. Catamaran (35-50 ft) best for stable sailing with 10-16 guests; from 1,200 USD full-day.
  3. Bowrider / deck boat (20-25 ft) perfect for couples or small families up to 6; from 350 USD half-day.
  4. Sailboat (35-45 ft) ideal for a relaxed experience with 4-8 people; from 500 USD half-day.
  5. Fishing boat (22-30 ft) purpose-built for angling with a crew for up to 6; from 450 USD half-day.

For a specific luxury model, check the Azimut 58 charter page on BoatHire24 for detailed specs and availability.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Cancun?

The high season runs from December to April, with calm seas and the easterly trade winds. May to November is the wet season, with higher humidity and a risk of tropical storms. However, the water temperature stays warm year-round. A local tip: if you book between August and October, you may find lower rates, but always check the marine forecast for northerly winds known as 'nortes' in winter. The best visibility for snorkelling is typically from March to June.

In summer, the sea is often glassy in the morning, but afternoon squalls can build quickly; always start your charter before 9 a.m. to guarantee the best conditions.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cancun?

No, for most charter boats with a skipper, a licence is not required. If you want to drive the boat yourself, you might need a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or a Mexican licence. For bareboat charters (without a skipper), the operator confirms exact requirements based on your nationality and the vessel type. Always verify with the owner when booking. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cancun or list your own.

At a glance

  • Price band: 80 to 2,500+ USD per trip
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Typical trip length: 4, 6 or 8 hours
  • Maximum capacity: 6 to 16 guests per boat
  • Currency: USD
  • Common departure: Puerto Cancún Marina

Questions about boat rental in Cancun

Can I rent a boat in Cancun for a day without a skipper?

Yes, some operators offer bareboat charters for experienced sailors, but you must hold a recognised licence and provide proof of experience.

What is the best area in Cancun for boating?

The Hotel Zone lagoon (Nichupté Lagoon) and the Caribbean coast near Isla Mujeres offer calm waters and stunning views.

Are drinks and food included in the rental price?

Many crewed charters include a lunch buffet and an open bar, but shared tours usually only provide snacks; check the listing details.

How far is Isla Mujeres from Cancun?

The distance from the entrance of the Nichupté Lagoon to the main dock on Isla Mujeres is about 8 nautical miles, roughly a 30-minute trip in a motorboat.

BoatHire24 simplifies your search for the perfect day on the water, whether you want a budget-friendly shared trip or a private luxury charter. If you own a boat in Cancun, you can also become a host on the platform and reach travellers from around the world. The marketplace handles bookings securely, giving you peace of mind and more time to enjoy the sea.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cancun and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
